title: Shields off/on comparison

summary.pageTitle: Capital One | Credit Cards, Checking, Savings & Auto Loans

conditions.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) HeadlessChrome/149.0.7827.55 Safari/537.36
conditions.scannerDisclosure: Automated Chromium scan from github-actions-ubuntu with browser 149.0.7827.55, timezone UTC, locale en-US, the listed viewport, and Brave Shields classification only. Treat results as reproducible evidence for this scan configuration, not a universal claim about all visitors.

warnings: This report is one automated, headless Chromium visit from a fixed en-US / UTC profile, with no scrolling, clicking, or consent interaction. Sites can behave differently for real users, browsers, regions, accounts, or network locations.

Counts are a lower bound: trackers that load only after interaction or consent, and any activity inside Web or Service Workers, are not observed. Service labels use a US-biased hand-curated catalog, so regional services may be under-labeled. Cookie and storage figures are an end-of-visit snapshot.
This scan typed a synthetic test value into 2 form fields (never submitting the form) to test whether typed input is captured and sent to third parties. The value is synthetic and is not stored.
Resolved 1 first-party subdomain that are CNAME aliases for third-party trackers (CNAME cloaking), which request-URL matching alone would miss.

warnings: This report is one automated, headless Chromium visit from a fixed en-US / UTC profile, with no scrolling, clicking, or consent interaction. Sites can behave differently for real users, browsers, regions, accounts, or network locations.

Brave Shields block simulation was enabled; matching requests were aborted before loading and are not included in request totals.
This scan typed a synthetic test value into 2 form fields (never submitting the form) to test whether typed input is captured and sent to third parties. The value is synthetic and is not stored.
Shields comparison runs should be collected under matched crawl conditions. Differences can still reflect timing, experiments, cache state, consent state, or bot detection.
